item.title: Foundering: A Brilliant Programmer and a Lot of Wild Living
item.description: After his murder, Bob Lee’s friends and family felt that his story was obscured by waves of headlines, rumors, and misinformation, first about street crime in San Francisco, then about the debaucherous lifestyle of the city's elite.

item.title: Netflix Plans to Buy Back Additional $25 Billion in Shares
item.description: Netflix Inc. announced plans to buy back $25 billion in stock after the company’s shares dropped following a weak financial outlook.

item.title: Gilts Slump as Buoyant Economic Data Backs Case for Rate Hikes
item.description: Gilts slumped after a rebound in the UK’s private sector backed the case for the Bank of England to raise interest rates this year.

item.title: Euro-Zone Economy Strained as War Impact Ripples Across Globe
item.description: The global economy just endured a second month of renewed price pressures but with less synchronized fallout on growth, according to the first take of business surveys for April.

item.title: UniCredit Raises Generali Stake After Earlier Signaling Cut
item.description: UniCredit SpA boosted its stake in Assicurazioni Generali SpA to 8.7%, despite earlier indications that it planned to trim the holding.

item.title: Thai IPO Pipeline Faces Difficulties, Exchange President Says
item.description: Thailand’s initial public offering pipeline continues to face challenges, even as signs of renewed investor interest have started to emerge, according to Stock Exchange of Thailand President Asadej Kongsiri.

item.title: Dip Buyers Are Running Back to Stocks Despite $100 Oil
item.description: The reflex to buy stocks is kicking back into gear.

item.title: Taiwan to Ease Limits on Active Funds’ Investments in TSMC
item.description: Taiwan’s financial regulator plans to ease the limit on how much funds can invest in a single stock, lifting an impediment that has prevented local money managers from taking full advantage of the surge in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.’s shares in recent years.

item.title: Honeywell Is Said to Near Sale of Warehouse Services Unit to AIP
item.description: Honeywell International Inc. is in advanced discussions to sell its warehouse and workflow solutions business to American Industrial Partners, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Intel’s $240 Billion Rally Slams Into a Potential Earnings Wall
item.description: Intel Corp. has been one of the hottest stocks in the market over the past 12 months, soaring 235% to the highest price since the dot-com bubble. But the rally is facing a potential roadblock in the company’s first-quarter earnings report due after the close Thursday.

item.title: China’s Major Credit Rating Firms to Meet on Improving Quality
item.description: China’s major credit‑rating firms are set to meet next week to discuss quality issues at the urging of regulators, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Goldman Strategist Sees European Earnings Pared by Weak Demand
item.description: European companies are set to see earnings growth of only a “few percent” in the first quarter, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s senior European strategist, substantially weaker than double-digit expansion in the US.

item.title: Singapore Blocks Access to Fake News Websites on Hostile Threat
item.description: Singapore ordered Internet service providers to block six foreign-linked, fake websites posing as local news sites as they could be used to carry out disinformation activities.

item.title: India’s Richest State Is Said to Prepare for Power Utility IPO
item.description: Maharashtra, India’s richest state, has begun preparations for an initial public offering of its power distribution utility, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Duterte Heads for Trial as ICC Confirms Drug War Charges
item.description: The International Criminal Court’s pre-trial chamber confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, setting the stage for a full-blown trial.

item.title: The Quiet AI Trade That’s Raking in Billions
item.description: A complex, long-overlooked part of the semiconductor world is now available to retail investors.

item.title: Ex-Samsung Manager Handed Six-Year Jail Sentence Over China Leak
item.description: A Korean court sentenced a former Samsung Electronics Co. engineer to more than six years in prison for leaking sensitive technology to a leading Chinese chipmaker, highlighting Seoul’s determination to protect the country’s trade secrets.

item.title: Push to Unwind Bitcoin Hoarder Shows Once-Hot Trade Collapsing
item.description: Shareholders in a UK Bitcoin accumulator are pushing it to sell all its tokens and wind down operations, one of the most dramatic examples of how the once-hot trade has fallen out of favor.

item.title: South Africa Ramps Up Fuel Imports From US as War Jolts Trade
item.description: South Africa is boosting oil-product imports from the US to help replace lost supplies from the Middle East, in another example of how the conflict is reshaping trade and threatening higher costs for consumers.

item.title: PwC Pays $166 Million to Settle HK Evergrande Audit Probe
item.description: PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP agreed to pay HK$1.3 billion ($166 million) in fines and compensation to settle investigations into its auditing work for the collapsed property giant China Evergrande Group.

item.title: Teck Warns of Higher Fuel Costs Caused by Mideast Energy Shock
item.description: Canada’s Teck Resources Ltd. warned of higher fuel costs for its flagship Chilean copper mines as the global mining industry grapples with supply-chain disruptions triggered by war in the Middle East.

item.title: Biggest Supermarket in the Baltics Plans Bond Market Return
item.description: Maxima Grupe UAB has been discussing a return to the bond market with investors after the Baltic food retailer repaid its only outstanding issue last year, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: UK Growth Rebounds as Firms Build Up Stocks, Surprise PMI Shows
item.description: Britain’s private sector quickly bounced back from a slowdown caused by the Iran war, according to a closely watched survey that also showed firms rushing to secure supplies and price pressures mounting.

item.title: Norway $2.2 Trillion Fund Lost 1.9% Led by Tech Stocks Slide
item.description: Norway’s wealth fund lost 1.9% in the first quarter, dragged down by investments in US technology stocks as the war in the Middle East rattled markets.

item.title: China Pledges to Stabilize Fertilizer Market as Planting Begins
item.description: China’s agriculture ministry said it will secure sufficient fertilizer supplies and stabilize prices, as the Iran war disrupts global markets for the key crop nutrient.

item.title: Deutsche Telekom’s CEO Lays Groundwork for Career-Defining Deal
item.description: For Deutsche Telekom AG Chief Executive Officer Timotheus Höttges, it’s the challenge of a lifetime — pulling off the largest M&A deal in history.

item.title: Google's Liz Reid on Who Will Own Search in a World of AI
item.description: Alphabet's head of search on adapting to the rise of LLMs.

item.title: Euro-Zone Private Sector Unexpectedly Shrinks as Services Slump
item.description: Business activity in the euro area unexpectedly shrank for the first time since late 2024 due to a steep drop in the services sector as the Iran war weighs on consumers.

item.title: Odd Lots: Google’s Liz Reid on Search in an AI World (Podcast)
item.description: Not too long ago, search engines were the dominant form of querying the internet. But that’s changing since the rise of large language models like ChatGPT, Claude, and Google’s Gemini. More and more people are getting their online info through AI, effectively bypassing the search bars of old and creating a tension for large tech companies that offer AI models, but also make money from web traffic and search-related advertising. In this episode, we speak with Elizabeth Reid, VP of search at Googl

item.title: Insider Trading Fugitive Stuck in China After US Flight Ban
item.description: A fugitive accused of insider trading says he’s trapped in China because the US won’t let him board a plane to Boston to fight the charges against him. The Justice Department isn’t sympathetic, saying he created the problem himself by fleeing from Singapore before he could be extradited.
